original_hypothesis.title: setRewards Can Retroactively Modify Past Epoch Rates Before Markets Are Updated
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Governance can call setRewards to change cantoPerBlock for any epoch, including past epochs. If update_market has not been called for a market since before the modified epoch, the next update_market call processes those historical blocks using the NEW cantoPerBlock value. This allows governance to retroactively increase or decrease rewards for specific epochs, with disproportionate impact on markets that haven't been recently updated.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: setRewards only checks epoch alignment, not whether the epoch is in the past or future. update_market's while loop reads cantoPerBlock[epoch] during catch-up. If cantoPerBlock was changed after the epoch passed but before update_market processed it, the new value is used retroactively.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setRewards writes cantoPerBlock[i] for any epoch range without checking if epoch is past; update_market reads cantoPerBlock[epoch] during catch-up loop

preconditions: Governance calls setRewards targeting past epochs
At least one market has lastRewardBlock before the modified epoch
Governance is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: cantoPerBlock[past_epoch] = new_value; update_market processes blocks in past_epoch using new_value instead of original
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Governance sets cantoPerBlock for past epochs to extreme values before stale markets are updated
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Specific markets receive inflated or deflated rewards based on retroactive rate changes

economic_questions: Is governance trusted to never modify past epoch rates?
How many markets are typically stale enough for this to matter?
Can this be combined with H001 (epochTime bug) for amplified impact?
validation_requirements: Set cantoPerBlock for a past epoch, call update_market, verify new rate is used
Compare rewards with original vs modified rates
Test if there's any protection against past-epoch modification

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to the full reward pool for modified epochs

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Depositors in markets affected by rate changes
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All pending rewards for modified epochs

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Governance trust model and setRewards usage patterns
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify past-epoch cantoPerBlock is mutable and affects update_market

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
